fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Florida Speech-Language Pathologist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a speech-language pathologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Florida - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: speech-language pathologist license reciprocity florida, how to transfer speech-language pathologist license to florida, speech-language pathologist compact florida, florida speech-language pathologist endorsement application

board.name: Board of Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology
board.mailing_address: 4052 Bald Cypress Way, Bin C-06, Tallahassee, FL 32399-3256

license_types.title: Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist
license_types.description: The full credential to independently practice speech-language pathology in Florida. Requires a master's degree, nine months of supervised professional employment, and a passing Praxis score.

license_types.title: Provisional SLP License
license_types.description: Issued to SLP graduates who have not yet passed the Praxis exam. Allows supervised practice for up to one year while completing remaining requirements.

steps.title: Graduate from an Accredited Program
steps.description: Earn a master's or doctoral degree in speech-language pathology from a program accredited by the Council on Academic Accreditation (CAA).

steps.title: Complete Supervised Clinical Practicum
steps.description: Complete a supervised clinical practicum that meets the requirements of your graduate program and the Florida Board of Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology.

steps.title: Pass the National Examination and Apply for Licensure
steps.description: Pass the Praxis Examination in Speech-Language Pathology and submit your application for licensure to the Florida Board of Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology.

documents.detail: Sent directly from the degree-granting institution to the Board office.
Score of 162 or higher on test 5331. Must be dated within three years of application.
Documentation of nine months of supervised professional employment experience.
Required as of July 1, 2025 (HB 975). Both initial applicants and renewals must comply.

telepractice_text: Florida permits telepractice for licensed SLPs. The practitioner must hold a valid Florida SLP license to provide services to patients located in Florida. Telepractice services must meet the same standard of care as in-person services. Florida does not currently participate in the ASLP Interstate Compact.
provisional_text: Florida issues a Provisional SLP License for graduates who have not yet passed the Praxis exam. This allows supervised practice for up to one year. The provisional licensee must work under the direct supervision of a fully licensed SLP in Florida.
reciprocity_text: Florida does not have formal reciprocity agreements. Out-of-state SLPs must apply for a new Florida license and meet all state-specific requirements. However, applicants holding the ASHA CCC-SLP may have some documentation requirements streamlined. Florida does not participate in the ASLP Interstate Compact.

faqs.question: How much does a Florida SLP license cost?
faqs.answer: The total initial licensure cost is approximately $180, which includes a $100 nonrefundable application fee and an $80 initial license fee. Biennial renewal is $80 if renewed before expiration.

faqs.question: What are the new fingerprinting requirements for Florida SLPs?
faqs.answer: Effective July 1, 2025, all healthcare professionals in Florida, including SLPs, must complete electronic fingerprinting and background screening for both initial licensure and renewal. This applies to HB 975 passed during the 2024 legislative session.

faqs.question: Can I practice SLP in Florida with a provisional license?
faqs.answer: Yes. Florida issues a Provisional SLP License for graduates who have not yet passed the Praxis exam. This allows supervised practice for up to one year while you complete remaining licensure requirements.

faqs.question: Does Florida have reciprocity for speech-language pathologists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers speech-language pathologist practice in Florida. To work in Florida, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my speech-language pathologist license to Florida?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 8 weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Florida while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Florida does not have formal reciprocity agreements. Out-of-state SLPs must apply for a new Florida license and meet all state-specific requirements. However, applicants holding the ASHA CCC-SLP may have some documentation requirements streamlined. Florida does not participate in the ASLP Interstate Compact.

hero.title: Become a Speech-Language Pathologist in Florida
hero.dek: Florida Speech-Language Pathologist license requires graduation from an accredited program, passing the national exam, and completion of a supervised professional experience.
